original_hypothesis.title: Whitelisted Delegate Can Overwrite Guard Storage Slot to Disable All Rental Protections
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Guard.checkTransaction allows delegatecall to whitelisted delegate targets. In delegatecall context, the called contract executes with the Safe's storage. A whitelisted delegate could overwrite the Safe's guard storage slot (Gnosis Safe internal slot) to address(0), permanently removing the guard. checkAfterExecution is empty and performs no post-execution integrity checks. Once the guard is removed, the renter can freely transfer all rented NFTs out of the Safe.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Delegatecall gives the callee full storage access. The guard only checks pre-execution (checkTransaction); checkAfterExecution is a no-op. No post-execution verification of guard/module integrity exists.
deployment_assumptions: A whitelisted delegate has the ability (intentionally or via vulnerability) to write to arbitrary storage slots
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Guard.sol checkAfterExecution is empty; Admin.sol toggleWhitelistDelegate has no code audit requirement

proof_of_signal.pattern: checkAfterExecution has empty function body - no post-execution integrity verification of guard slot or module state

preconditions: A delegate contract is whitelisted by ADMIN_ADMIN
The delegate contract can write to arbitrary storage slots (vulnerability or intentional)
Renter calls delegatecall to the whitelisted delegate

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: checkAfterExecution is no-op; delegatecall gives full SSTORE access
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Delegatecall overwrites guard slot to address(0); all subsequent Safe transactions bypass guard
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Renter steals all rented NFTs from Safe = total value of all rented assets

economic_questions: Which contracts are currently whitelisted as delegates?
Do any whitelisted delegates have arbitrary storage write capability?
Would checkAfterExecution with guard verification mitigate this?
validation_requirements: Deploy delegate that writes to guard storage slot, whitelist it, execute via Safe
Verify guard is removed after delegatecall
Confirm NFT transfers succeed without guard

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Full value of all rented NFTs in the Safe

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All lenders with active rentals in the compromised Safe
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All rented assets in Safes using the vulnerable delegate

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Audit all currently whitelisted delegates for storage write capability
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test guard removal via delegatecall to custom contract

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
